title: Daily Market Digest: June 10, 2026
subtitle: Market sentiment weighed down by geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Lower Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Surge
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are trading lower today as escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, coupled with higher-than-expected inflation data, dampen investor sentiment. Technology shares are facing particular weakness.

items.headline: Nuvalent Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: Biotech company Nuvalent surged after GSK agreed to acquire it for $10.6 billion.

items.headline: J.M. Smucker Posts Strong Earnings
items.summary: The J.M. Smucker Co. jumped after reporting a stronger profit for the latest quarter than analysts expected.

items.headline: Cracker Barrel Jumps on Q3 Beat
items.summary: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store surged in premarket trading after reporting strong fiscal third-quarter earnings and raising its full-year profitability guidance.

items.headline: Chewy Climbs on Solid Q1 Results
items.summary: Chewy, Inc. climbed after announcing robust financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, with net sales up 7.7% to $3.36 billion and diluted earnings per share of $0.23.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Falls on Financing Deals
items.summary: Super Micro Computer tumbled after announcing $7 billion in equity-related financing deals to cover the cost of hardware component purchases for AI server orders.

items.headline: Uranium Energy Corp. Plunges on Wider Loss
items.summary: Uranium Energy Corp. plunged after reporting a wider-than-expected adjusted loss of $0.07 per share for its third fiscal quarter.

items.headline: United Natural Foods Drops on Earnings Miss
items.summary: United Natural Foods Inc. plummeted after its third-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings of $0.77 per share lagged consensus estimates.

items.headline: Old Dominion Slumps as Amazon Expands Freight
items.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line slumped after Amazon expanded its Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) freight offering to all destinations in the U.S.

items.headline: May CPI Rises to 4.2% Annually, Highest in Three Years
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for May rose 0.5% on a seasonally adjusted basis, pushing the annual inflation rate to 4.2%. This marks the highest annual rate in three years and was significantly driven by a 3.9% monthly jump in energy prices.

items.headline: FOMC Meeting Looms, Rate Hike Expectations Build
items.summary: With inflation accelerating, the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on June 16-17 will be closely watched. Markets are now leaning towards a potential rate hike later in 2026, marking a shift from earlier expectations of rate cuts.

items.headline: Tech Sector Experiences Profit-Taking Amid AI Cost Concerns
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly AI-related names like $NVDA, $AAPL, and $AMD, saw significant profit-taking and declines today. Investors are growing nervous about the high costs associated with artificial intelligence investments and are seeking clearer proof of profitability.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge as U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates
items.summary: Oil prices climbed today, with Brent crude reaching $91.78 per barrel and U.S. crude at $88.31, as escalating military actions between the U.S. and Iran raise concerns about global energy supply chains and demand.

items.headline: Rotation in ASX: Tech and Energy Lead, Defensives Lag
items.summary: In the Australian Stock Exchange (ASX) session, institutional capital aggressively rotated into high-beta growth sectors like Information Technology (+2.15%) and Energy (+1.14%), while defensive sectors such as Utilities (-1.87%), Real Estate (-1.50%), and Consumer Staples (-0.44%) suffered declines.
